title: Currency Management: Accounts, Convertibility, and Internationalization
focus: Mechanics of Vostro/Nostro accounts, currency convertibility status in India, steps for Rupee internationalization, and Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER).
rationale: This section covers foundational macroeconomic concepts regarding currency exchange and RBI's strategic initiatives for the Rupee, which are highly examinable in UPSC economics.
sectionExcerpt: Vostro Account: A foreign bank's account held in a domestic bank in domestic currency. Nostro Account: A domestic bank's account held in a foreign bank in foreign currency. Currency convertibility has two components: Current Account (fully convertible in India) and Capital Account (partially convertible). Steps for Internationalization of Rupee include RBI's Strategic Action Plan 2024-25, SPECTRA Project, linking UPI, and ACU settlement. REER measures a country's currency value against a basket of currencies adjusted for inflation.
evidenceBullets: A Vostro account is a foreign bank's account held in a domestic bank in the domestic currency (e.g., a US bank holding an INR account in SBI).
A Nostro account is a domestic bank's account held in a foreign bank in the foreign currency.
In India, the Current Account is fully convertible, while the Capital Account is partially convertible.
The SPECTRA Project is a software platform designed to streamline External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) and Trade Credits reporting and approval.
REER is derived by adjusting the Nominal Effective Exchange Rate (NEER) for relative consumer prices; a lower REER indicates the currency is undervalued and can potentially boost exports.

title: Remittances and the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS)
focus: India's remittance inflow statistics, economic significance, and the regulatory framework of the Liberalised Remittance Scheme.
rationale: Remittances are a critical component of India's external sector. The LRS has specific eligibility criteria and limits that are prime targets for objective questions.
sectionExcerpt: Remittances inflow to India doubled to USD 118.7 billion in 2023-24. Top source countries: US, UAE, UK. Top recipient states: Maharashtra, Kerala, Tamil Nadu. India tops the global list of recipient countries. Remittances accounted for over 10% of India's gross current account inflows and surpass gross inward FDI. Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS), introduced in 2004 by RBI, allows resident individuals (including minors) to remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year. It is not available to corporates, partnership firms, HUFs, or Trusts.
evidenceBullets: India is the top recipient country for remittances globally in 2024, ahead of Mexico.
The top source country for remittances to India in 2023-24 was the US (27.7%), and the top recipient state was Maharashtra (20.5%).
Remittances provide a stable source of financing for India, surpassing gross inward foreign direct investment (FDI).
The Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) allows resident individuals, including minors, to freely remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year.
The LRS is strictly not available to corporates, partnership firms, Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs), or Trusts.

title: Free Trade Agreements: India-UK FTA
focus: Conclusion of the India-UK Free Trade Agreement.
rationale: A brief but distinct update on India's bilateral trade agreements.
sectionExcerpt: India and the United Kingdom (UK) announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) after three years of formal talks.
evidenceBullets: India and the United Kingdom (UK) have announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) following three years of formal negotiations.
